baseline.notes: Run 49 Rush field_crop only: F1 0.417, keeper 0.430, highlights 0.446. Run 53 with ball_crop dual-view regressed it badly (keeper 0.321, highlights 0.359). Adaptive auto-detection failed (player heights nearly identical between Rush and sporting_ac, 47 vs 43 px). New approach: per-job ball_crop_enabled flag in the job submission API. Run 54 omits the flag (defaults to BALL_CROP_ENABLED=false in env), so Rush should run with field_crop only and recover Run 49's reel-weighted numbers.

targets.notes: Rush should match Run 49 numerically (or very close — same code path, same env). Per-reel: keeper F1 ~0.43, highlights ~0.45. If results don't match, the per-job override wiring is broken.

ball_crop_enabled (env).reason: Default off after Run 53 regression on Rush. Per-job flag opts in for zoomed-out cameras.

ball_crop_enabled (job).reason: No override — defers to env-var default = false. Rush gets field_crop only.

notes: Run #54 (resubmitted at d4fe075): first submission (job cd6bbd4b, commit 87884f9) regressed Rush to F1 0.358 despite ball_crop being OFF. Root cause: commit 20f862f permanently added a 'WIDE+ZOOM dual-view' prefix to the prompt; when ball_crop is off the VLM still gets that prefix and over-fires throw_in (which matches the prefix's 'ball-in-hands' cue). Throw_in flags 126->214, shot_on_target -21, catch -22, etc. d4fe075 splits _DUAL_VIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX out and prepends only when ball_crop is active. Resubmitted as job 6b7ad58b. Expected: matches Run 49 baseline F1 0.417 / keeper 0.430 / highlights 0.446.
